arts.ttl: Real Madrid: Vinicius Junior clarifies dramatic Ballon d'Or boycott and links with big money Saudi Arabia move
arts.sum: The Brazilian finished second in the 2024 Ballon d’Or voting

arts.ttl: Vinicius Jr sends Real Madrid transfer message amid £200m Saudi approach
arts.sum: Vinicius is entering the final two years of his contract at Real Madrid with the Brazilian having been the subject of a £200million bid from the Saudi Pro League

arts.ttl: Two Real Madrid stars to return to XI for Atlético clash
arts.sum: An insight into the line-up plans of Real Madrid head coach for the club’s upcoming meeting with Atlético has on Monday evening been provided. The latest edition of the Madrid…

arts.ttl: Real Madrid watching Palace's Wharton - Tuesday's gossip
arts.sum: Real Madrid are watching Adam Wharton, Liverpool to face Joshua Kimmich competition and Bart Verbruggen on Chelsea's radar.

arts.ttl: Official: Real Madrid’s Player of the Month for February
arts.sum: The winner of the Player of the Month award at La Liga giants Real Madrid has on Monday been unveiled online. And player to have scooped the prize? Brahim Díaz.…

arts.ttl: Sources: Ex-Atleti SD Berta in talks with Arsenal
arts.sum: Andrea Berta has emerged as a candidate to become Arsenal's next sporting director, sources have told ESPN.

Rüdiger, Tchouameni, Camavinga, Modric and Endrick will all be watching their step at the Bernabéu with the threat of suspension also hanging over Real Madrid coach Carlo Ancelotti, who has also seen two yellows (in the games against Stuttgart and Brest). Real Madrid are the team with the second most yellow cards so far in this Champions League.

arts.ttl: Atletico Madrid predicted lineup vs Real Madrid - Champions League
arts.sum: Atletico Madrid's predicted lineup for the first leg of their Champions League round of 16 tie against Real Madrid.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid fined for homophobic Guardiola chants
arts.sum: Real Madrid are fined by Uefa after some of their fans directed homophobic chants towards Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola in their Champions League second-leg tie at the Bernabeu.
